la-crdf-lynwood.q: Can you notarize family law documents for women at CRDF Lynwood?
la-crdf-lynwood.a: Yes. We frequently notarize child custody, guardianship, visitation, divorce, and support-related documents for women in custody at CRDF Lynwood when a notarized signature is required.

la-crdf-lynwood.q: What information do you need to schedule a CRDF notary visit?
la-crdf-lynwood.a: We need the inmate’s full legal name, booking number if available, a description of the documents that need to be notarized, and any deadlines or upcoming court dates.

la-crdf-lynwood.q: Do you coordinate with attorneys for CRDF notary appointments?
la-crdf-lynwood.a: Yes. We routinely coordinate with family law and criminal defense attorneys to ensure documents are ready to sign and meet court requirements before the notary visit at CRDF Lynwood.

la-crdf-lynwood.q: How long does a CRDF Lynwood notary appointment take?
la-crdf-lynwood.a: Plan for 1–2 hours including travel, security screening, wait time, and the actual signing. The notarization itself is quick, but jail security procedures add time.

la-crdf-lynwood.q: Can you notarize real estate or financial documents at CRDF?
la-crdf-lynwood.a: Yes. We can notarize deeds, loan documents, lease agreements, bank forms, and other financial paperwork, as long as the documents are prepared in advance and comply with California notary law.

la-mdc.q: Is MDC Los Angeles the same as Twin Towers or Men\u2019s Central Jail?
la-mdc.a: No. Metropolitan Detention Center Los Angeles is a federal Bureau of Prisons facility that holds federal inmates, while Twin Towers and Men\u2019s Central Jail are Los Angeles County jails.

la-mdc.q: Is the process the same as visiting a county jail?
la-mdc.a: No. MDC Los Angeles is a federal Bureau of Prisons facility with a more involved approval process for professional visitors. Federal visits require advance paperwork, attorney involvement, and approval that can take several business days.

la-mdc.q: Does the inmate\u2019s attorney need to be involved?
la-mdc.a: In most cases, yes. Federal facilities typically require notary visits to be coordinated through or sponsored by the inmate\u2019s attorney of record.

la-mdc.q: How far in advance should I schedule an MDC notary appointment?
la-mdc.a: We recommend contacting us at least one week before your deadline so we can complete Bureau of Prisons approval steps and schedule your appointment without rushing.

la-mdc.q: How do I confirm someone is at MDC Los Angeles?
la-mdc.a: You can search the Federal Bureau of Prisons inmate locator at bop.gov/inmateloc using the inmate\u2019s name or register number, or contact MDC Los Angeles directly at (213) 485-0439.

la-mdc.q: Can you work with out-of-state attorneys or consulates?
la-mdc.a: Yes. We frequently coordinate MDC notary appointments with federal defense attorneys from other states and with consular officials to meet court and agency requirements.

la-mens-central.q: How do I find someone's booking number at Men's Central Jail?
la-mens-central.a: You can search the LA County Sheriff's Inmate Information Center online at app5.lasd.org/iic using the inmate's name and date of birth. The booking number helps confirm their location and coordinate the visit.

la-mens-central.q: What if the inmate was just transferred to MCJ?
la-mens-central.a: After a transfer, it can take 24–48 hours for an inmate to be fully processed and available for professional visits. We recommend confirming their status before scheduling if there has been a recent transfer.

la-mens-central.q: Can family members bring documents to the jail notary appointment?
la-mens-central.a: No — professional notary visits are separate from regular family visitation. You will need to get documents to us before the appointment, and we will bring them into the facility.

la-mens-central.q: What ID does the inmate need for notarization at MCJ?
la-mens-central.a: MCJ maintains booking records with photos that can be used for identification purposes. If the inmate has a valid California ID, driver's license, or passport on file, that can also be used.

la-mens-central.q: How long does a Men's Central Jail notary appointment take?
la-mens-central.a: Plan for 1–2 hours total including travel, security screening, wait time, and the actual signing. The notarization itself is quick, but jail procedures add time.

la-mens-central.q: What if the inmate refuses to sign the documents?
la-mens-central.a: A notary cannot force anyone to sign a document. If the inmate refuses or is unable to sign, the notarization cannot be completed. You may still be responsible for a trip fee in this situation.

la-nccf.q: How do I confirm someone is at North County Correctional Facility?
la-nccf.a: You can search the LA County Sheriff's Inmate Information Center online at app5.lasd.org/iic using the inmate's name and date of birth. The system will list the current facility, including North County if that is where they are housed.

la-nccf.q: Can you visit both North County and another Pitchess unit on the same day?
la-nccf.a: In some situations, yes. If you have documents for inmates at both North County and another Pitchess unit, let us know. We can discuss whether it is practical to coordinate both visits on the same trip, subject to facility schedules and approval.

la-nccf.q: Are visiting rules different at North County?
la-nccf.a: North County is a maximum-security facility with its own visiting rules and procedures for professional visits. We confirm current requirements with the facility to ensure your appointment follows their guidelines.

la-nccf.q: How far in advance should I schedule an NCCF notary appointment?
la-nccf.a: Because of security and travel time, we recommend contacting us at least a few days in advance when possible. For urgent matters, call or text as soon as you know you need the appointment and we will review available options.

la-nccf.q: What ID does the inmate need for notarization at NCCF?
la-nccf.a: NCCF maintains booking records with photos that can be used for identification purposes. If the inmate has a valid California ID, driver's license, or passport on file, that can also be used. We confirm ID options before the appointment.

la-nccf.q: What if the inmate is transferred to another facility?
la-nccf.a: Inmates can be transferred between Castaic and downtown facilities. We recommend confirming the inmate's location close to the appointment time. If they have been moved, we can usually reschedule for the new facility with an updated quote if travel distance changes.

la-pitchess.q: How do I know if someone is at Pitchess or North County?
la-pitchess.a: You can search the LA County Sheriff's Inmate Information Center online at app5.lasd.org/iic using the inmate's name and date of birth. The system will list the current facility, which may be one of the Pitchess units or North County Correctional Facility.

la-pitchess.q: Can you visit more than one Castaic facility on the same trip?
la-pitchess.a: In some cases, yes. If you have documents for inmates housed at different Castaic facilities, let us know. We can discuss whether it is practical to coordinate multiple visits on the same day, subject to facility schedules.

la-pitchess.q: Do Castaic facilities have different visiting rules than downtown LA jails?
la-pitchess.a: Yes. Each facility has its own schedule and rules for professional visits. For example, some units may allow professional visits daily from 8:00 AM to 10:00 PM, while others may limit visits to weekdays or specific hours. We confirm the current procedures for the specific unit before your appointment.

la-pitchess.q: How far in advance should I schedule a Pitchess notary appointment?
la-pitchess.a: Because of the travel time and coordination involved, we recommend contacting us at least a few days in advance when possible. For urgent matters, call or text as soon as you know you need the appointment and we will review available options.

la-pitchess.q: What ID does the inmate need at Pitchess?
la-pitchess.a: Castaic facilities maintain booking records with photos that can be used for identification purposes. If the inmate has a valid California ID, driver's license, or passport on file, that can also be used. We confirm ID options before the appointment.

la-pitchess.q: What if the inmate is moved back to downtown Los Angeles?
la-pitchess.a: Inmates can be transferred between Castaic and downtown LA facilities. We recommend confirming the inmate's location close to the appointment time. If they have been moved, we can usually reschedule for the new facility with an updated quote if travel distance changes.

la-twin-towers.q: How is Twin Towers different from Men\u2019s Central Jail?
la-twin-towers.a: Twin Towers and Men\u2019s Central Jail are adjacent facilities at the same downtown LA complex, but they have separate entrances, visiting procedures, and housing units. Twin Towers is the nation\u2019s largest mental health facility and also houses the Inmate Reception Center (IRC) where new bookings are processed. We confirm which facility the inmate is actually housed in before scheduling.

la-twin-towers.q: How do I find someone\u2019s booking number at Twin Towers?
la-twin-towers.a: You can search the LA County Sheriff\u2019s Inmate Information Center online at app5.lasd.org/iic using the inmate\u2019s name and date of birth. The system will show which facility they are currently housed at. You can also call the 24-hour inmate information line at (213) 473-6080.

la-twin-towers.q: What if the inmate is in medical or mental health housing at Twin Towers?
la-twin-towers.a: Inmates in medical or mental health housing may have restricted visiting access. We coordinate with the facility to determine if a professional notary visit is possible and what additional steps may be required.

la-twin-towers.q: Can I schedule for both Twin Towers and Men\u2019s Central in one trip?
la-twin-towers.a: If you have documents for inmates at both facilities, let us know. Since they are adjacent, we may be able to coordinate both visits on the same day, though each facility requires separate check-in and security procedures.

la-twin-towers.q: What ID does the inmate need at Twin Towers?
la-twin-towers.a: Twin Towers maintains booking records with photos that can be used for identification purposes. If the inmate has a valid California ID, driver\u2019s license, or passport on file, that can also be used. We confirm ID options before the appointment.

la-twin-towers.q: What if the inmate is transferred before the Twin Towers notary appointment?
la-twin-towers.a: Inmates can be transferred between facilities with little notice. We recommend confirming the inmate\u2019s location close to the appointment time. If they have been moved, we can often reschedule for the new facility with an updated quote if travel distance changes.

oc-central.q: Where is the James A. Musick Facility located?
oc-central.a: The James A. Musick Facility is located at 13502 Musick Road, Irvine, CA 92618, in south Orange County near the junction of the I-5 and I-405 freeways.

oc-central.q: What type of inmates are housed at Musick?
oc-central.a: Musick primarily houses low-risk inmates, including those convicted of minor offenses and non-violent misdemeanors. It houses both male and female inmates and has a capacity of approximately 1,250 beds.

oc-central.q: How do I confirm someone is at the Musick Facility?
oc-central.a: You can search the Orange County Sheriff's Department inmate locator online or call the facility at (949) 855-2600 with the inmate's name and date of birth.

oc-central.q: What ID does the inmate need for notarization at Musick?
oc-central.a: The facility maintains booking records with photos that can be used for identification. Acceptable government-issued IDs include a California driver's license, California ID card, U.S. passport, alien registration card, or military ID, when available in the inmate's records.

oc-central.q: Is the visiting environment different from other jails?
oc-central.a: Yes. Musick is a minimum-security facility, so the visiting environment is generally more relaxed than at maximum-security jails. Visits may take place at benches rather than behind glass, and professional visitors often have more flexible access.

oc-central.q: How long should I plan for a Musick notary appointment?
oc-central.a: Plan for 2 to 3 hours total including travel from Los Angeles to south Irvine, check-in, wait time, and the signing. The notarization itself is quick, but the distance from LA adds significant travel time.

oc-musick.q: Where is the James A. Musick Facility located?
oc-musick.a: The James A. Musick Facility is located at 13502 Musick Road, Irvine, CA 92618, in south Orange County near the junction of the I-5 and I-405 freeways.

oc-musick.q: What type of inmates are housed at Musick?
oc-musick.a: Musick primarily houses low-risk inmates, including those convicted of minor offenses and non-violent misdemeanors. It houses both male and female inmates and has a capacity of approximately 1,250 beds.

oc-musick.q: How do I confirm someone is at the Musick Facility?
oc-musick.a: You can search the Orange County Sheriff's Department inmate locator online or call the facility at (949) 855-2600 with the inmate's name and date of birth.

oc-musick.q: What ID does the inmate need for notarization at Musick?
oc-musick.a: The facility maintains booking records with photos that can be used for identification. Acceptable government-issued IDs include a California driver's license, California ID card, U.S. passport, alien registration card, or military ID, when available in the inmate's records.

oc-musick.q: Is the visiting environment different from other jails?
oc-musick.a: Yes. Musick is a minimum-security facility, so the visiting environment is generally more relaxed than at maximum-security jails. Visits may take place at benches rather than behind glass, and professional visitors often have more flexible access.

oc-musick.q: How long should I plan for a Musick notary appointment?
oc-musick.a: Plan for 2 to 3 hours total including travel from Los Angeles to south Irvine, check-in, wait time, and the signing. The notarization itself is quick, but the distance from LA adds significant travel time.

oc-theo-lacy.q: Where is the Theo Lacy Facility located?
oc-theo-lacy.a: The Theo Lacy Facility is located at 501 The City Drive South, Orange, CA 92868, between the Santa Ana Freeway (I-5) and the Garden Grove Freeway (SR-22). Parking is available at the North parking structure across from the Lamoreaux Justice Center.

oc-theo-lacy.q: What type of inmates are housed at Theo Lacy?
oc-theo-lacy.a: Theo Lacy is a maximum-security facility with a capacity of approximately 3,100 to 3,400 inmates. It houses both sentenced and pre-sentenced male inmates arrested for misdemeanors and felonies.

oc-theo-lacy.q: How do I confirm someone is at the Theo Lacy Facility?
oc-theo-lacy.a: You can search the Orange County Sheriff's Department inmate locator online or call the facility at (714) 935-6940 with the inmate's name and date of birth.

oc-theo-lacy.q: What ID does the inmate need for notarization at Theo Lacy?
oc-theo-lacy.a: The facility maintains booking records with photos that can be used for identification. If the inmate has a valid California ID, driver's license, or passport on file, that can also be used. We confirm ID options before the appointment.

oc-theo-lacy.q: Are professional visits different from regular visiting hours at Theo Lacy?
oc-theo-lacy.a: Yes. Professional visitors such as attorneys and notaries typically have broader access than public visitors, who are limited to Friday through Monday. We confirm the current professional visiting schedule with the facility before each appointment.

oc-theo-lacy.q: How long does a notary appointment at Theo Lacy take?
oc-theo-lacy.a: Plan for 1 to 3 hours total including travel from Los Angeles, security screening, wait time, and the signing. The notarization itself is quick, but facility procedures and travel from LA add time.

riv-benoit-indio.q: Where is the John J. Benoit Detention Center located?
riv-benoit-indio.a: The John J. Benoit Detention Center is located in Indio, California, serving the Coachella Valley communities of Indio, Palm Desert, Coachella, and surrounding areas.

riv-benoit-indio.q: What types of inmates are housed at the Indio jail?
riv-benoit-indio.a: The facility houses a mix of pre-trial and sentenced inmates facing misdemeanor and felony charges out of Riverside County courts, including cases from Indio and nearby cities.

riv-benoit-indio.q: How do I confirm someone is at the John J. Benoit Detention Center?
riv-benoit-indio.a: You can use the Riverside County Sheriff's inmate locator online or contact the county's inmate information line with the person's name and date of birth to confirm they are housed at the Indio facility.

riv-benoit-indio.q: What ID does the inmate need for notarization at Indio jail?
riv-benoit-indio.a: The facility maintains booking records with photos that can be used for identification. If the inmate has a valid California ID, driver's license, or passport on file, that can also be used. We confirm ID options with the jail before the appointment.

riv-benoit-indio.q: How long does a notary appointment at the Indio jail take?
riv-benoit-indio.a: Because of the distance from Los Angeles and facility procedures, plan for several hours total including travel, security screening, wait time, and the signing itself. The notarization portion is quick, but logistics add time.

riv-benoit-indio.q: Can you handle urgent or court-date-driven requests at Indio?
riv-benoit-indio.a: In many cases, yes. If you have a court date or filing deadline, let us know when you first reach out. We will review travel time, facility scheduling, and our calendar to see what options are available.

riv-larry-smith.q: Where is the Larry D. Smith Correctional Facility located?
riv-larry-smith.a: The Larry D. Smith Correctional Facility is located at 1627 S Hargrave Street, Banning, CA 92220, near the I-10 freeway in the San Gorgonio Pass area, approximately 1.5 hours from Los Angeles.

riv-larry-smith.q: How do I confirm someone is at Larry D. Smith Correctional Facility?
riv-larry-smith.a: You can search the Riverside County Sheriff's inmate locator online or call the facility at (951) 922-7300 with the inmate's name and date of birth to confirm they are housed at Smith Correctional.

riv-larry-smith.q: How are visits scheduled at Larry D. Smith Correctional Facility?
riv-larry-smith.a: You can call (951) 922-7540 between 9:00 AM and 5:00 PM daily to schedule visits, and visits must be scheduled at least one day in advance. For attorney or professional visits, the facility is contacted at (951) 922-7300 to coordinate directly.

riv-larry-smith.q: What are the check-in rules for visiting Smith Correctional?
riv-larry-smith.a: Visitor check-in begins 60 minutes before the scheduled visit and closes 20 minutes before the start time. Visitors who arrive during the final 20 minutes before the visit will not be allowed to check in, so arriving early is essential.

riv-larry-smith.q: What ID does the inmate need for notarization at Smith Correctional?
riv-larry-smith.a: The facility maintains booking records that can be used for identification. If the inmate has a valid California ID, driver's license, or passport on file, that can also be used. ID options are confirmed with the facility before the appointment.

riv-larry-smith.q: How long should I plan for a Larry D. Smith Correctional notary appointment?
riv-larry-smith.a: Plan for 4 to 5 hours total including travel from Los Angeles (about 1.5 hours each way), security screening, wait time, and the signing. The notarization itself is quick, but travel and facility procedures add significant time.

riv-robert-presley.q: Where is the Robert Presley Detention Center located?
riv-robert-presley.a: The Robert Presley Detention Center (RPDC) is located at 4000 Orange St, Riverside, CA 92501, in downtown Riverside. It is approximately 60 to 80 minutes from Los Angeles depending on traffic.

riv-robert-presley.q: What is RPDC\u2019s role within Riverside County jails?
riv-robert-presley.a: The Robert Presley Detention Center serves as one of the primary booking and detention facilities for Riverside County, handling initial booking, pre-trial detention, and court appearances for the downtown Riverside courthouse area.

riv-robert-presley.q: How do I confirm someone is at the Robert Presley Detention Center?
riv-robert-presley.a: You can search the Riverside County Sheriff's inmate locator online or call (951) 955-4500 with the inmate's name and date of birth to confirm they are housed at RPDC.

riv-robert-presley.q: How are visits scheduled at Robert Presley Detention Center?
riv-robert-presley.a: You can call (951) 341-8888 between 9:00 AM and 5:00 PM daily to schedule visits, and visits must be scheduled at least one day in advance. For attorney or professional visits, the facility is contacted directly at (951) 955-4500 to coordinate scheduling.

riv-robert-presley.q: What ID does the inmate need for notarization at RPDC?
riv-robert-presley.a: The facility maintains booking records that can be used for identification. If the inmate has a valid California ID, driver's license, or passport on file, that can also be used. ID options are confirmed with the facility before the appointment.

riv-robert-presley.q: What about parking at the Riverside jail?
riv-robert-presley.a: RPDC is in downtown Riverside. Metered parking is available on surrounding streets, and two public parking structures are at the corner of Lemon and 12th Street. Extra time should be allowed for parking and walking to the facility.

riv-robert-presley.q: How long should I plan for a Riverside jail notary appointment?
riv-robert-presley.a: Plan for 3 to 4 hours total including travel from Los Angeles (60 to 80 minutes each way), security screening, wait time, and the signing. The notarization itself is quick, but travel and facility procedures add time.

riv-southwest.q: Where is the Southwest Detention Center located?
riv-southwest.a: The Southwest Detention Center is located at 30755-B Auld Rd, Murrieta, CA 92563, in the French Valley area between Murrieta and Temecula. It is approximately 1.5 hours from Los Angeles via the I-15 freeway.

riv-southwest.q: Is Southwest Detention Center the same as the Cois M. Byrd Detention Center?
riv-southwest.a: Yes. The Southwest Detention Center and Cois M. Byrd Detention Center are the same facility, and it may be listed under either name in Riverside County inmate locator systems.

riv-southwest.q: How do I confirm someone is at Southwest / Cois M. Byrd?
riv-southwest.a: You can search the Riverside County Sheriff's inmate locator online or call (951) 696-3050 with the inmate's name and date of birth to confirm they are housed at Southwest Detention Center.

riv-southwest.q: How are visits scheduled at Southwest Detention Center?
riv-southwest.a: You can call (951) 696-3470 between 9:00 AM and 5:00 PM daily to schedule visits, and visits must be scheduled at least one day in advance. For attorney or professional visits, contact the facility at (951) 696-3050.

riv-southwest.q: Does Southwest Detention Center house male and female inmates?
riv-southwest.a: No. Southwest Detention Center houses male inmates only. For female inmates in Riverside County, check the Robert Presley Detention Center or contact the Riverside County Sheriff's Department for guidance.

riv-southwest.q: How long should I plan for a Southwest Detention Center notary appointment?
riv-southwest.a: Plan for 4 to 5 hours total including travel from Los Angeles (about 1.5 hours each way via I-15), security screening, wait time, and the signing. The notarization itself is quick, but travel and facility procedures add time.

sb-central.q: Where is the Central Detention Center located?
sb-central.a: The Central Detention Center (CDC) is located at 630 E Rialto Ave, San Bernardino, CA 92415, in downtown San Bernardino. It is approximately 60 to 75 minutes from Los Angeles.

sb-central.q: What is CDC's role within San Bernardino County jails?
sb-central.a: The Central Detention Center serves primarily as a booking and short-term housing facility for San Bernardino County. Inmates booked at CDC may be transferred to West Valley Detention Center, Glen Helen Rehabilitation Center, or High Desert Detention Center after initial processing and classification.

sb-central.q: How do I confirm someone is at the Central Detention Center?
sb-central.a: You can search the San Bernardino County Sheriff's inmate locator online or call (909) 386-0969 with the inmate's name and date of birth to confirm they are housed at CDC.

sb-central.q: What are the public visiting hours at CDC?
sb-central.a: Public visiting hours at the Central Detention Center are Wednesday through Saturday, 8:30 AM to 7:00 PM, by appointment only. Visits must be scheduled at least one day in advance by calling (909) 887-0364 or scheduling online. Attorneys and bail bond agents may visit any day of the week.

sb-central.q: How is the inmate's identity verified for notarization at CDC?
sb-central.a: The facility maintains booking records with photos that can be used for identification. If the inmate has a valid California ID, driver's license, or passport on file, that can also be used. ID options are confirmed with the facility before the appointment.

sb-central.q: How long should I plan for a Central Detention Center notary appointment?
sb-central.a: Plan for 2.5 to 3.5 hours total including travel from Los Angeles to downtown San Bernardino, security screening, wait time, and the signing. The notarization itself is quick, but travel and facility procedures add time.

sb-glen-helen.q: Where is Glen Helen Rehabilitation Center?
sb-glen-helen.a: Glen Helen Rehabilitation Center is located at 18000 Institution Rd, Devore, CA 92407, approximately 12 miles northwest of downtown San Bernardino and about 60 to 80 minutes from Los Angeles via I-15.

sb-glen-helen.q: How do I find out if someone is at Glen Helen?
sb-glen-helen.a: You can search the San Bernardino County Sheriff's inmate locator online or call (909) 708-8371 with the inmate's name and date of birth to confirm they are housed at Glen Helen.

sb-glen-helen.q: What are the visiting hours at Glen Helen?
sb-glen-helen.a: Public visiting hours at Glen Helen Rehabilitation Center are Wednesday through Saturday, 8:30 AM to 7:00 PM, by appointment only. Visits must be scheduled at least one day in advance by calling (909) 887-0364. Attorneys may visit any day of the week with advance notice.
Yes. Glen Helen has separate male and female facilities on the same campus. The female facility has a capacity of approximately 326 inmates and houses both pre-trial and sentenced women.

sb-glen-helen.q: What ID does the inmate need for notarization at Glen Helen?
sb-glen-helen.a: The facility maintains booking records with photos that can be used for identification. If the inmate has a valid California ID, driver's license, or passport on file, that can also be used. Identification options are confirmed with the facility before the appointment.

sb-glen-helen.q: How long does a notary appointment at Glen Helen take?
sb-glen-helen.a: Plan for 3 to 4 hours total including travel from Los Angeles to Devore, security screening, wait time, and the signing. Glen Helen's more remote location adds travel time compared to closer facilities.

sb-high-desert.q: Where is High Desert Detention Center?
sb-high-desert.a: High Desert Detention Center is located at 9438 Commerce Way, Adelanto, CA 92301, in the High Desert region of San Bernardino County.

sb-high-desert.q: How do I find out if someone is at High Desert Detention Center?
sb-high-desert.a: You can search the San Bernardino County Sheriff's inmate locator online or call the facility with the person's name and date of birth for inmate information.

sb-high-desert.q: Do you notarize documents at other San Bernardino County jails?
sb-high-desert.a: Yes. In addition to High Desert Detention Center, we visit West Valley Detention Center, Glen Helen Rehabilitation Center, and Central Detention Center for mobile notary appointments.

sb-high-desert.q: What ID does the inmate need for notarization at High Desert Detention Center?
sb-high-desert.a: The facility maintains booking and inmate records, including photos and facility-issued inmate ID, which can often be used for identification under California notary law. If a valid California ID, driver's license, passport, or other acceptable ID is on file, we may be able to rely on that as well. Identification options are confirmed with the facility before the appointment.

sb-high-desert.q: How long does a notary appointment at High Desert Detention Center take?
sb-high-desert.a: Plan for 2 to 4 hours total including travel to Adelanto, security screening, wait time, and the signing itself. The notarization is quick, but the remote location and jail procedures add time.

sb-high-desert.q: What if the inmate was just booked or is being transferred?
sb-high-desert.a: Newly booked inmates may not be available for professional visits until processing is complete. If the inmate is being transferred between San Bernardino County facilities, it is best to confirm their location and housing unit close to the appointment time.

sb-west-valley.q: Where is West Valley Detention Center located?
sb-west-valley.a: West Valley Detention Center (WVDC) is located at 9500 Etiwanda Ave, Rancho Cucamonga, CA 91739, off the I-15 freeway, approximately 50 to 70 minutes from Los Angeles depending on traffic.

sb-west-valley.q: How large is West Valley Detention Center?
sb-west-valley.a: WVDC is one of the largest county jails in California with a bed capacity of 3,347, processing between 50,000 and 60,000 bookings and releases each year and housing primarily pre-trial inmates.

sb-west-valley.q: How do I confirm someone is at West Valley Detention Center?
sb-west-valley.a: You can search the San Bernardino County Sheriff's inmate locator online or call (909) 708-8371 with the inmate's name and date of birth to confirm they are housed at WVDC.

sb-west-valley.q: What are the visiting hours at West Valley Detention Center?
sb-west-valley.a: Public visiting hours at WVDC are Wednesday through Saturday, 8:30 AM to 7:00 PM, by appointment only. Visits must be scheduled at least one day in advance by calling (909) 887-0364 or using the online scheduling system. Attorneys may visit any day of the week.

sb-west-valley.q: How is the inmate's identity verified for notarization at WVDC?
sb-west-valley.a: The facility maintains booking records with photos that can be used for identification. If the inmate has a valid California ID, driver's license, or passport on file, that can also be used. Identification options are confirmed with the facility before the appointment.

sb-west-valley.q: How long should I plan for a West Valley Detention Center notary appointment?
sb-west-valley.a: You should plan for 2 to 3 hours total including travel from Los Angeles to Rancho Cucamonga, security screening, wait time, and the signing. The notarization itself is quick, but travel and jail procedures add time.

sba-main-jail.q: Where is the Santa Barbara County Main Jail located?
sba-main-jail.a: The Santa Barbara County Main Jail is located at 4436 Calle Real, Santa Barbara, CA 93110. It is the county's primary jail and houses male and female inmates awaiting trial, sentencing, or serving county jail sentences.

sba-main-jail.q: Do mobile notaries work at the Santa Barbara Main Jail?
sba-main-jail.a: Yes. Independent mobile notaries regularly provide services at the Santa Barbara County Main Jail. The notary checks in with custody staff, is escorted to a non-contact visiting area, and completes the notarization with the inmate.

sba-main-jail.q: How is the inmate's identity verified for notarization at the Main Jail?
sba-main-jail.a: Custody staff maintain booking records with photos and identifying information that can be used to confirm the inmate's identity. If the inmate has a valid government-issued ID on file, that can also be used. ID options are confirmed with the facility before the appointment.

sba-main-jail.q: How long should I plan for a Santa Barbara Main Jail notary appointment?
sba-main-jail.a: Plan for 3 to 5 hours total, including travel from Los Angeles, check-in with custody staff, escort to and from the visiting area, wait time, and the signing itself. The notarization portion is quick, but facility procedures and distance add time.

sba-main-jail.q: Is the Northern Branch Jail the same as the Main Jail?
sba-main-jail.a: No. The Main Jail on Calle Real in Santa Barbara and the Northern Branch Jail on Black Road in Santa Maria are separate facilities about 65 miles apart. Both are operated by the Santa Barbara County Sheriff's Office, so it is important to confirm which facility the inmate is housed at before scheduling.

sba-main-jail.q: How do I confirm someone is at the Santa Barbara County Main Jail?
sba-main-jail.a: You can search the Santa Barbara County Sheriff's Office inmate locator online or call the Main Jail at (805) 681-4260 with the inmate's name and date of birth.

sba-northern-branch.q: Where is the Northern Branch Jail located?
sba-northern-branch.a: The Northern Branch Jail is located at 2301 Black Road, Santa Maria, CA 93455. It is a 376-bed Santa Barbara County facility that houses male and female inmates in medium and high-security housing units.

sba-northern-branch.q: Can a mobile notary visit the Northern Branch Jail?
sba-northern-branch.a: Yes. Independent mobile notaries can provide services at the Northern Branch Jail. The notary coordinates with the watch commander, checks in with custody staff, and completes the notarization during a professional visit.

sba-northern-branch.q: What are the visiting hours and scheduling rules at Northern Branch?
sba-northern-branch.a: The Northern Branch Jail offers visits Sunday through Saturday, 8:00 AM to 5:00 PM. Visits are scheduled through the ViaPath online registration system. Appointments start on the hour, and visitors should check in 15 to 30 minutes before their scheduled time.

sba-northern-branch.q: How many visits are inmates allowed at Northern Branch Jail?
sba-northern-branch.a: Inmates at the Northern Branch Jail are generally allowed up to four visits per week. All visitors must be on the inmate's approved visitor list and must check in with the same photo ID used to register.

sba-northern-branch.q: Is the Northern Branch Jail the same as the Santa Barbara County Main Jail?
sba-northern-branch.a: No. The Northern Branch Jail in Santa Maria and the Main Jail on Calle Real in Santa Barbara are separate facilities about 65 miles apart. Both are operated by the Santa Barbara County Sheriff's Office, and many social visits for county inmates are handled at the Northern Branch.

sba-northern-branch.q: How long should I plan for a Northern Branch Jail notary appointment?
sba-northern-branch.a: Plan for 5 to 7 hours total, including travel from Los Angeles to Santa Maria (approximately 2.5 hours each way), check-in with custody staff, wait time, and the signing itself. The notarization portion is quick, but the distance adds significant travel time.

sba-northern-branch.q: How do I confirm someone is at the Northern Branch Jail?
sba-northern-branch.a: You can search the Santa Barbara County Sheriff's Office inmate locator online or call the Northern Branch Jail at (805) 554-3100 with the inmate's name and date of birth.

vn-east-valley.q: Where is East Valley Jail located?
vn-east-valley.a: East Valley Jail is located at 2101 E Olsen Rd, Thousand Oaks, CA 91360. It is approximately 45 minutes from Los Angeles via US-101.

vn-east-valley.q: How do I confirm someone is housed at East Valley Jail?
vn-east-valley.a: You can search the Ventura County Sheriff's inmate locator online or call (805) 371-8163. If the inmate is not listed at East Valley, they may be at Ventura County Main Jail or Todd Road Jail.

vn-east-valley.q: What are the visiting days and policies at East Valley Jail?
vn-east-valley.a: Visiting schedules at East Valley Jail are set by the Ventura County Sheriff's Office and may differ from other county facilities. Contact the facility or call us to confirm current visiting days, visit length, and visitor limits.

vn-east-valley.q: What can I bring into East Valley Jail during a visit?
vn-east-valley.a: Only your personal key ring, photo ID, and one baby bottle or blanket if bringing an infant. No cell phones, wallets, purses, backpacks, food, beverages, cameras, or reading materials are allowed. The facility will not hold items and there are no storage lockers.

vn-east-valley.q: How is East Valley Jail different from Ventura County Main Jail or Todd Road Jail?
vn-east-valley.a: East Valley Jail is a minimum-security facility that houses lower-classification inmates and those nearing release. Ventura County Main Jail in Ventura primarily handles booking and pre-trial detainees, while Todd Road Jail in Santa Paula primarily houses sentenced inmates in longer-term custody.

vn-east-valley.q: How long should I plan for an East Valley Jail notary appointment?
vn-east-valley.a: Plan for 2 to 3 hours total including travel from Los Angeles (approximately 45 minutes each way via US-101), security screening, wait time, and the signing.

vn-main-jail.q: Where is Ventura County Main Jail located?
vn-main-jail.a: The Ventura County Main Jail, also known as the Pre-Trial Detention Facility, is located at 800 S Victoria Ave, Ventura, CA 93009. It is approximately 65 minutes from Los Angeles via US-101.

vn-main-jail.q: How do I confirm someone is housed at Ventura County Main Jail?
vn-main-jail.a: You can search the Ventura County Sheriff's inmate locator online or call (805) 654-3335. Be sure to confirm whether the inmate is at the Main Jail or at Todd Road Jail in Santa Paula.

vn-main-jail.q: What are the visiting days and sessions at Ventura County Main Jail?
vn-main-jail.a: Visiting days are Wednesday, Thursday, Saturday, and Sunday. Sessions are typically morning (8:30 AM – 11:30 AM), afternoon (1:30 PM – 4:30 PM), and evening (7:00 PM – 9:00 PM), assigned by housing unit. Check-in starts 1 hour before each session and closes 30 minutes before the session ends.

vn-main-jail.q: What can I bring into Ventura County Main Jail during a visit?
vn-main-jail.a: Only your personal key ring, photo ID, and one baby bottle or blanket if bringing an infant. No cell phones, wallets, purses, backpacks, food, beverages, cameras, or reading materials are allowed. The facility will not hold items and there are no storage lockers.

vn-main-jail.q: What is the difference between Ventura County Main Jail and Todd Road Jail?
vn-main-jail.a: The Main Jail (Pre-Trial Detention Facility) in Ventura primarily houses pre-trial detainees and handles initial booking. Todd Road Jail in Santa Paula primarily houses sentenced inmates and those in longer-term custody. They are separate facilities with different addresses and visiting schedules.

vn-main-jail.q: How long should I plan for a Ventura County Main Jail notary appointment?
vn-main-jail.a: Plan for 3 to 4 hours total including travel from Los Angeles, security screening, wait time, and the signing. The drive from Los Angeles to Ventura County Main Jail is approximately 65 minutes each way via US-101.

vn-todd-road.q: Where is Todd Road Jail located?
vn-todd-road.a: Todd Road Jail is located at 600 Todd Road, Santa Paula, CA 93060. It is approximately 70 minutes from Los Angeles via US-101.

vn-todd-road.q: How do I confirm someone is housed at Todd Road Jail?
vn-todd-road.a: You can search the Ventura County Sheriff's inmate locator online or call (805) 654-3335. Be sure to confirm whether the inmate is at Todd Road or the Main Jail in Ventura, as they are separate facilities.

vn-todd-road.q: What are the visiting days and check-in rules at Todd Road?
vn-todd-road.a: Visiting days are Wednesday, Thursday, Saturday, and Sunday. Each visit is 30 minutes, with two visits per inmate per week. Check-in starts 1 hour before and closes 30 minutes before the session ends.

vn-todd-road.q: What can I bring into Todd Road Jail during a visit?
vn-todd-road.a: Only your personal key ring, photo ID, and one baby bottle or blanket if bringing an infant. No cell phones, wallets, purses, backpacks, food, beverages, cameras, or reading materials are allowed. The facility will not hold items and there are no storage lockers.

vn-todd-road.q: What is the difference between Todd Road Jail and the Ventura County Main Jail?
vn-todd-road.a: The Main Jail in downtown Ventura primarily houses pre-trial detainees and handles initial booking. Todd Road in Santa Paula primarily houses sentenced inmates and those in longer-term custody, with different visiting schedules and housing units.

vn-todd-road.q: How long should I plan for a Todd Road notary appointment?
vn-todd-road.a: Plan for 3 to 4 hours total including travel from Los Angeles, security screening, wait time, and the signing. Todd Road is approximately 70 minutes each way via US-101.
